title: General's quarters
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Unidentified observation chamber}}

{{Quote|Welcome to the general's quarters.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] to [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]|<ref name="LoE">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>}}
The '''General's Quarters''' was a large [[Observation deck/Legends|observation chamber]] situated at the aft section of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] flagship, [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']], atop a [[Wizard's Tower/Legends|fin-like spire]].

==Description==
{{Quote|The Chancellor's signal is coming from right there. The observation platform at the top of that spire.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="RotS" />}}
The General's quarters was an observation chamber located on the top section of a spire known as the Wizard's Tower onboard the Separatist [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|''Providence''-class carrier/destroyer]] ''Invisible Hand''.<ref name="RotS" /> It was a fully equipped military command center with tactical displays, duty posts, a briefing table<ref name="tfc-db">{{DB|starship|tradefederationcruiser|Trade Federation cruiser}}</ref> and windows with computer programming that was able to make any battle that look place around the ship completely visible to anyone looking through them. Next to it was a chair known as the General's chair.<ref name="RotS Novel">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]</ref> Up a staircase on the other side of the room was a platform that spanned the length of the room, up to the windows. The platform contained a door to a turbolift lobby.<ref name="RotS" />

==History==
===The Battle of Coruscant===
{{Quote|Do you have a location...? In the General's quarters. Any sign of Grevious himself?|Obi-Wan Kenobi to R2-D2|<ref name="RotS Novel" />}}
Grievous brought [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to the General's quarters and shackled him to the General's chair after kidnapping him from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]]. Inside it, he recorded and broadcast a [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] message to the citizens of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], gloating over the capture of Palpatine.<ref name="LoE" /> Later, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] boarded the ''Invisible Hand'' to rescue Palpatine. Before they arrived, Count [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and Palpatine discussed their plan, which Dooku believed involved him killing Kenobi when the Jedi arrived, causing Skywalker to fall to the dark side, after which he would take Dooku captive. Dooku hid inside a turbolift shortly before Kenobi and Skywalker entered the turbolift lobby.<ref name="RotS Novel" />

[[File:Dookuepisodeiii.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Count Dooku fighting in the General's quarters.]]
The Jedi cut a hole in the door to the General's quarters with their lightsabers<ref name="RotS Novel" /> and made their way down the staircase, where they greeted the Chancellor. Palpatine spotted spotted Dooku entering the General's quarters flanked by two [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]]<ref name="RotS" /> and several [[OOM-series security droid/Legends|OOM security droids]],<ref name="RotS Comic">[[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith (comic series)|''Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith'' comic adaptation]]</ref> to which he alerted the Jedi. Dooku jumped over the railing of the platform and drew his [[Dooku's lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]; the Jedi doing the same. The Count asked Kenobi and Skywalker to hand over their lightsabers, claiming that he didn't want to make a mess in front of the Chancellor, but Kenobi responded that they weren't going to let Dooku escape and ignited his, followed by Skywalker and Dooku. The trio fought Dooku up the stairs and onto the platform, with Kenobi taking out the battle droids in the process. On top of the platform, Dooku kicked aside Skywalker and lifted Kenobi into the air with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], [[Telekinesis/Legends|choking]] him. Skywalker watched as he threw Kenobi under the platform across the room and brought the walkway down on top of him, knocking Kenobi unconscious. In response, Skywalker got up and kicked Dooku over the railing, before jumping over it himself to continue fighting him.<ref name="RotS" />

Skywalker and Dooku eventually made their way back towards the General's chair, where Skywalker bested Dooku, cutting off both of his hands. The Jedi grabbed the Sith's lightsaber and ignited it as Dooku fell to his knees, with his head in between Skywalker's blade and his own. Palpatine commended Skywalker for his victory and ordered him to kill Dooku, who looked at him in surprise. Skywalker hesitated, but Palpatine continued goading him, and the Jedi gave in, cutting off Dooku's head and sheathing the lightsabers. Skywalker freed the Chancellor, who reassured him that Dooku was too dangerous to be left alive and the pair began to head towards the turbolift lobby. As they passed Kenobi, Skywalker bent down to check on him and determined that he was still living. Palpatine told Skywalker to leave him, fearing that dragging Kenobi with them would slow them down and cause them to be killed, but the latter informed the former that if Kenobi died, so would they.<ref name="RotS" />

With Skywalker carrying Kenobi on his back, the Jedi and the Chancellor entered the turbolift lobby. Skywalker tried to open the turbolift but found it wasn't active, so he asked [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] over his [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to turn on the turbolift. Before R2-D2 could turn it on, the ''Invisible Hand''{{'s}} artificial gravity was damaged by an attack from the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Guarlara (Venator-class)/Legends|Guarlara]]'' and the direction from which Palpatine and the two Jedi had came from began drawing them downwards. The pair barely managed to open the turbolift doors and climb into the shaft before the gravity became fully tiled at 90 degrees. Skywalker and Palpatine began running down the turbolift shaft, through the Wizard's Tower.<ref name="RotS" />

[[File:Handsplits.png|thumb|right|200px|The ''Invisible Hand'' breaking in half, destroying the General's quarters.]]
Not long later, the ''Invisible Hand'' began plummeting towards Coruscant and the ship broke in half. The General's quarters were destroyed, along with the rest of the spire and the ship's aft section as it broke away and burned up in Coruscant's atmosphere.<ref name="tfc-db" /><ref name="ROTS VD">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>
